subject: Databases
title: Expression Indexes
description: A micro-lesson that makes Expression Indexes usable.

hook.text: WHERE LOWER(email) = 'ada@test.com' can't use a regular index on email. The function blocks it.

buildup.text: An expression index indexes the result of a function: CREATE INDEX ON users(LOWER(email)).

discovery.text: Now WHERE LOWER(email) = ... uses the index. The indexed expression must match the query expression exactly.

twist.text: Expression indexes work with any immutable function. JSON field extraction, date parts, string transforms.

climax.text: Indexing JSONB fields: CREATE INDEX ON events((data->>'type')). Query JSON efficiently without changing schema.

punchline.text: Expression indexes: index the computed value you actually query.

quiz.question: Why can't a regular index on email support WHERE LOWER(email) = ...?
options: The index stores raw email values, not the lowercased version the query needs
Regular indexes don't support text columns
The LOWER function is too slow for indexing
